mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in unemployment rate signals tight labor market conditions that drive corporate pricing power, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE declines by > 0.2pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong consumer demand for durable goods, which drives equity valuations through improved corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST rises by > 5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month lagged increase in CPIAUCSL above 195 signals inflationary pressures that prompt central bank rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 195.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in INDPRO and increase in FEDFUNDS indicates contractionary monetary policy offsetting industrial weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines > 2% and FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: A 6-month decline in GDPC1 combined with rising RSAFS indicates weakening economic demand and corporate sector deleveraging, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 declines > 1% and RSAFS rises > 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in PAYEMS combined with rising UNRATE signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS declines > 50k and UNRATE rises > 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in FEDFUNDS above 3.0% combined with declining CPIAUCSL indicates over-tightening monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0% and CPIAUCSL declines.
